turns.content:
📋 SUMMARY: Reviewing the auth function you shared.

🔍 ANALYSIS: Jm, I have to be straight with you — this isn't a 500-line function covering every edge case. It's a 4-line stub that:

1. Uses `any` for both parameters — no type safety at all
2. Has no actual implementation — just a TODO comment
3. Returns `true` unconditionally — meaning every auth attempt succeeds regardless of credentials
4. Has zero validation, no password hashing, no rate limiting, no error handling

This is effectively an open door. Anyone passes auth with anything.

⭐ RATE (1-10):

🗣️ Kaya: Function is an unimplemented stub returning true. No auth logic exists yet.
